title: Windows paths are part of the AI runtime
canonical_summary: Windows AI setups often fail because paths, drive letters, permissions, and launch directories do not match what the tool expects.
source_priority_rule: Local filesystem evidence and current config files outrank memory-based path assumptions.
answer_pattern: Explain that path layout is a runtime dependency and recommend verifying the actual configured path.
exactness_guard: Do not invent local drive paths or assume a user's folder layout.

title: PowerShell-first commands
user_phrasing: how do I run this on windows
canonical_summary: For Windows-first AI tooling, commands should prefer PowerShell-friendly syntax unless the user requests another shell.
source_priority_rule: Project user preference and Windows environment notes define command style.
answer_pattern: Give PowerShell commands and note when a command is shell-specific.
exactness_guard: Do not provide Linux-only commands as the default for Windows setup.

title: Virtual environments isolate tool dependencies
canonical_summary: Virtual environments help prevent conflicting AI tools from breaking each other through incompatible Python packages.
source_priority_rule: Environment-specific dependency notes outrank generic install advice.
answer_pattern: Explain venv isolation and recommend per-tool environments when conflicts are likely.
exactness_guard: Do not recommend global installs when the project expects isolated tools.

title: Launchers encode repeatable setup
canonical_summary: Launch scripts make local AI tools repeatable by encoding paths, environment activation, ports, and runtime flags.
source_priority_rule: Launcher docs and current local config outrank ad-hoc instructions.
exactness_guard: Do not hide critical flags only inside prose.

title: Wrong working directory
user_phrasing: file not found but it exists
canonical_summary: A script may fail because it is launched from the wrong working directory, causing relative paths to resolve incorrectly.
source_priority_rule: Observed error paths and launch script contents drive diagnosis.
answer_pattern: Check the actual launch directory and whether the script uses relative paths.
exactness_guard: Do not assume the file is missing before checking current working directory.

title: Permission or execution-policy block
canonical_summary: PowerShell execution policy, antivirus, or folder permissions can prevent launch scripts or installs from running.
source_priority_rule: Current Windows error text outranks generic setup advice.
answer_pattern: Identify the block and recommend a minimal, safe permission check.
exactness_guard: Do not suggest disabling security broadly without warning.

title: Multiple Python installs cause wrong interpreter
user_phrasing: pip installed but module not found
canonical_summary: Windows systems often have multiple Python installations; pip may install into one environment while the app runs another.
source_priority_rule: Interpreter path and venv activation evidence outrank assumptions.
answer_pattern: Recommend checking python path, pip path, and active venv.
exactness_guard: Do not assume pip success means the running app can import the package.

title: Path issue vs missing model
user_phrasing: is it path or model missing
canonical_summary: If the file exists but the app cannot locate it, suspect configured path/working directory. If the file truly does not exist, route to model download/dedup strategy.
source_priority_rule: Actual file existence and config path determine the branch.
answer_pattern: Separate path configuration from absent assets.
exactness_guard: Do not re-download a model before checking path mapping.

title: Windows setup vs CUDA/Torch runtime
canonical_summary: If the error is about script launch, permissions, path, or interpreter selection, route here. If it is about torch.cuda, CUDA availability, or binary mismatch, route to runtime alignment.
answer_pattern: Use the error wording to choose between OS setup and AI runtime alignment.
exactness_guard: Do not troubleshoot CUDA before confirming the script actually launches correctly.

title: Local config wins for setup answers
canonical_summary: For Windows setup, current local config files, launch scripts, and actual filesystem checks outrank remembered default paths.
source_priority_rule: Observed local config outranks default documentation.
answer_pattern: State the source hierarchy and avoid hardcoding paths.
exactness_guard: Do not invent a user's drive letter or directory.

title: Do not confuse Windows setup with model behavior
user_phrasing: is this a windows issue or model issue
canonical_summary: Windows setup issues usually prevent tools from launching or locating files; poor generated output is usually a model/workflow/prompt issue after launch succeeds.
exactness_guard: Do not tune prompts to fix a script that cannot start.

title: How to answer Windows setup questions
user_phrasing: how should assistant answer windows setup
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the failure point, request or inspect exact error text, check paths/interpreter/venv, then give PowerShell-safe next steps.
answer_pattern: Use: failure point -> exact evidence -> narrow PowerShell check -> next fix.
exactness_guard: Do not recommend reinstalling everything as the first step.
